Job Title: Chief of Staff to CFO - Business Analyst / Commercial Analyst
Ad Text:
Chief of Staff to CFO - Business Analyst / Commercial Analyst

Start your career where it actually makes an impact.

At 1KOMMA5°, we’re building the energy company of the future—helping households transition to clean, affordable energy powered by sun and wind.

We’re looking for a Business Analyst early in their career, who’s strong with numbers, commercially curious, and ready to step out of purely technical work into a role where your insights directly influence real business decisions.

If you’re currently in investment banking, Big 4, consulting, or a graduate commercial role, this is your chance to accelerate your career in a fast-moving, high-growth environment.

The Role:

As Chief of Staff, you'll work closely with the CFO to support analysis, reporting, and commercial decision-making across the business. The role will assist in ensuring strategic projects are appropriately analyzed, achievable and beneficial to the company from a financial persepective. It is ideal for someone who wants to learn fast, take ownership early, and gain exposure across multiple areas of a growing company.

What You’ll Be Doing:
Manage and implement strategic projects across Finance, Legal, Procurement, Sales, and Operations
Act as a key coordination point between the CFO, Finance team, internal stakeholders, and external partners
Prepare executive-level reports, presentations, and decision-making materials, and ensure follow-up on actions
Identify and implement process improvements to increase efficiency and scalability across business functions
Support financial, commercial, and operational analysis, including KPI tracking and ad-hoc reporting
Assist the CFO in prioritising and managing key workstreams to ensure focus on high-impact initiatives
About You:
1–3 years minimum experience in investment banking, Big 4, consulting, or a business analyst/commercial analyst role
Degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or similar
Strong Excel skills and confidence working with data
Solid analytical thinking with structured way of working
Clear communicator who can explain numbers in a simple way
Proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take ownership
Organised and able to manage multiple tasks
Bonus Points:
Exposure to financial modelling or forecasting
Experience with PowerPoint, dashboards, or BI tools
Interest in renewable energy or sustainability
Why Join Us?

Be part of a globally recognised renewables company that is driving positive change and making a real impact on the future of energy.
North Sydney location, working very closely with the Australian CFO.
Collaborative and innovative company culture, where your ideas and expertise will be valued and recognised.
Reporting directly to the CFO, on offer here is a base salary of up to $120k + super, commesurate with experience.  

To be considered for this position send through an application today. 


Insight Resourcing
www.insightresourcing.com.au

Job Title: Principal Planning Analyst, Strategy and Planning Team
Company: Cairns & Hinterland Hospital and Health Service
Category:
Strategy & Planning (Consulting & Strategy)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About us:

The Strategy and Planning team provide strategic leadership, strategic direction and coordination for planning initiatives across Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health Service, to meet the current and future health care needs of our community.

Cairns Hospital is the largest major hospital in Far North Queensland, caring for patients from the Mossman/Port Douglas to Tully in the south and out to Croydon in the west. 

Our population is 260,000 with First Nations people making up 14% of the region's population. Cairns Hospital provides a range of specialist outpatient, inpatient, and emergency services from its CBD site on the Cairns Esplanade. The hospital has 506 inpatient beds and 53 emergency department treatment spaces, 16 ICU beds, 22 special care nursery beds, 48 inpatient mental health beds and 25 maternity beds.

Cairns offers a widely-varied lifestyle in a tropical environment boasting several local landmarks and is the perfect base from which to explore the tropical north. Popular activities include exploring the World Heritage-listed Great Barrier Reef and the Daintree rainforest, as well as the Atherton Tablelands to the west of town and the Port Douglas region to the north. Cairns is also a well-known venue for sporting events, such as mountain biking competitions and marathon festivals.

We're committed to improving the health and wellbeing of people in Far North Queensland by providing high quality acute health care services.

About your role:

Extract large data sets from multiple sources, analyse and interpret results to inform planning requirements for population health projections, local needs assessment, geographical or health infrastructure planning, specific population group planning, health demand and scenario impact projections.
Undertake research and environmental scanning activities to proactively support the delivery of innovative, enhanced/ alternative models of care, or service re-orientation in line with local, state, and national directives.
Manage and coordinate designated data analysis projects, undertaking responsibility for planning, scoping, and scheduling activities, and completing projects in accordance with agreed timeframes and deliverables.
Provide analysis and feedback on Federal and State policy and planning documents, such as guidelines, frameworks, benchmarks and ensure their correct application when producing planning artefacts.

For more information about this role, please refer to the attached Role Description.

Vaccine Preventable Diseases (VPD) evidence as required for your employment in accordance with legislation/government policy and Directives.

What we offer:

Cairns and Hinterland Hospital and Health service offer a number of workplace benefits. Besides attractive remuneration, generous superannuation, salary packaging and career development opportunities, we value a healthy work/life balance that allows you take advantage of the sunshine, hiking tracks, swimming spots, reef and rainforest. 

Learn what it's like to work with us, in an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Discover our dynamic and culturally diverse region through community and lifestyle.

Listen to what some of our staff have to say about working in our regions.

Visit our website to learn more about our hospitals and health centres.
